title: Penalties for Offenses under 2016 Seabased Rules
content:
1. Refusal to condone or renegotiate a loan incurred by the seafarer after premature termination of employment contract through no fault of the seafarer:
- 1st Offense: Suspension of License (2 to 6 months)
- 2nd Offense: Suspension of License (6 months and 1 day to 1 year)
- 3rd Offense: Suspension of License (1 year and 1 day to 2 years)
- 4th Offense: Cancellation of License

2. Default on contractual obligations to the principal/employer:
- 1st Offense: Suspension of License (2 to 6 months)
- 2nd Offense: Suspension of License (6 months and 1 day to 1 year)
- 3rd Offense: Suspension of License (1 year and 1 day to 2 years)
- 4th Offense: Cancellation of License

3. Failure to submit reports on serious incidents (piracy, death, missing seafarer, serious illness/injury requiring repatriation):
- 1st Offense: Suspension of License (2 to 6 months)
- 2nd Offense: Suspension of License (6 months and 1 day to 1 year)
- 3rd Offense: Suspension of License (1 year and 1 day to 2 years)
- 4th Offense: Cancellation of License

Light Offenses and Penalties:
1. Owner/partner/officer of licensed manning agency becoming officer/member of board of travel agency:
- 1st Offense: Reprimand
- 2nd Offense: Fine PhP20,000
- 3rd Offense: Suspension of License (1 to 3 months)
- 4th Offense onwards: Suspension of License (3 to 6 months)

2. Inducing seafarer to transfer employment unless to liberate from oppressive terms:
- Same penalties as above

3. Influencing any person/entity not to employ seafarer who did not apply through agency or is union-affiliated:
- Same penalties as above

4. Failure to deploy contracted seafarer within prescribed period without valid reason:
- Same penalties as above

5. Coercing seafarer to accept prejudicial arrangements in exchange for benefits:
- Same penalties as above

6. Disregard of orders, notices, legal processes issued by Administration:
- Same penalties as above

7. Failure to submit reports on employment status, vacancies, remittance, departures, resignations/separations:
- Same penalties as above

8. Violation of other relevant laws, rules, guidelines on recruitment and placement of seafarers:
- Same penalties as above

Additional Provisions:
- Money claims from recruitment violations may be awarded in addition to administrative penalties.
- Section 124: In lieu of suspension, a fine of PhP50,000 per month of suspension may be imposed.
- Section 125: Cancellation of license penalty applies if offense involves five or more workers in a single case.
- Section 126: Motion for reconsideration is treated as an appeal.
